---
title: "Method or Technique Name"
description: "One-line summary of what this method does and when to use it"
date: 2026-01-01
date-modified: last-modified
categories: [methods, Bayesian, Python]
---

::: {.note-meta}
🌱 Seedling · Last tended April 2026
:::

## When to use this

The decision context. Under what conditions is this the right tool? What alternatives exist and when do they win instead?

## The core idea

Explain the method. Start intuitive, then get precise. Use diagrams or equations as needed:

$$
p(\theta \mid y) \propto p(y \mid \theta) \, p(\theta)
$$

## Implementation

Working code you've tested. Annotate decisions — future you will forget why you set that prior.

```python
import pymc as pm

with pm.Model() as model:
    # Prior choice: weakly informative
    beta = pm.Normal("beta", mu=0, sigma=2.5)
    # Likelihood
    y_obs = pm.Bernoulli("y_obs", logit_p=beta, observed=data)
    trace = pm.sample(1000)
```

## Pitfalls

Things that broke when you tried them, common mistakes, or subtleties the textbook glosses over.

## Resources

- [Textbook or tutorial](https://example.com) — one-line annotation of why it's useful
- [Package documentation](https://example.com)
- [Blog post that clarified something](https://example.com)

## Related

- [Concept this method serves](/notes/concepts/related.qmd)
- [Paper that introduced or validated this](/notes/papers/author-year.qmd)
- [Project where you applied this](/projects/project-name/index.qmd)

::: {.backlinks}
### Linked from
- [Page that links here](/path/to/page.qmd)
:::
